sections.label: Section 858
sections.original: οἰωνὸν ἐθέμην καλλίνικα σὰ στέφη ·
sections.translation: I take your crown of victory as an omen . We , as you know , are exposed to the waves

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: δορὸς Δαναϊδῶν , καὶ μέγας Θήβαις ἀγών .
sections.translation: of war with the Danaids , and great is the struggle for Thebes . Eteocles , our king , is already gone in full armor to meet Mycenae ’ s champions ; and he has bidden me inquire of you our best course to save the city .

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: Ἐτεοκλέους μὲν οὕνεκʼ ἂν κλῄσας στόμα
sections.translation: For Eteocles I would have closed my lips and refrained from all response , but to you I will speak , since it is your wish to learn . This country , Creon , has been long afflicted , ever since Laius became a father against the will of the gods , begetting hapless Oedipus to be his own mother ’ s husband .

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: αἵ θʼ αἱματωποὶ δεργμάτων διαφθοραὶ
sections.translation: That bloody destruction of his eyes was planned by the gods as an example to Hellas ; and the sons of Oedipus went foolishly astray in wishing to throw over it the veil of time — as if they could outrun the gods ! For by robbing their father of his due honor

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: οὔτʼ ἔξοδον διδόντες , ἄνδρα δυστυχῆ
sections.translation: and allowing him no freedom , they exasperated the poor sufferer ; so he , suffering and disgraced as well , breathed dreadful curses against them . And I , because I left nothing undone or unsaid , incurred the hatred of the sons of Oedipus .

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: ἐγγὺς δὲ θάνατος αὐτόχειρ αὐτοῖς , Κρέον ·
sections.translation: But death inflicted by each other ’ s hands awaits them , Creon ; and the many heaps of the slain , some from Argive , some from Theban spears , shall cause bitter lamentation in the land of Thebes . Alas for you , poor city , you are being involved in their ruin ,

sections.label: Section 885
sections.original: εἰ μὴ λόγοισι τοῖς ἐμοῖς τὶς πείσεται .
sections.translation: unless I can persuade one man . The best course was to prevent any child of Oedipus becoming either citizen or king in this land , on the ground that they were under a ban and would overthrow the city . But since evil has the mastery of good , there is

sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: μίʼ ἔστιν ἄλλη μηχανὴ σωτηρίας .
sections.translation: one other means of safety ; but — for it is unsafe for me to tell , and painful too for those whose fortune it is to supply their city with the saving cure — I will go away . Farewell ; among the rest

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: τὸ μέλλον , εἰ χρή , πείσομαι · τί γὰρ πάθω ;
sections.translation: I will endure what is to come , if I must ; for what else can I do ?

sections.label: Section 896
sections.original: ἐπίσχες αὐτοῦ , πρέσβυ .
sections.translation: Stay here , old man .

sections.label: Section 896b
sections.original: μὴ ʼπιλαμβάνου .
sections.translation: Do not catch hold of me .

sections.label: Section 897
sections.original: μεῖνον , τί φεύγεις ;
sections.translation: Wait ; why do you try to escape ?

sections.label: Section 897b
sections.original: ἡ τύχη σʼ , ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: It is your fortune that tries to escape you , not I .

sections.label: Section 898
sections.original: φράσον πολίταις καὶ πόλει σωτηρίαν .
sections.translation: Tell me what can save Thebes and her citizens .

sections.label: Section 899
sections.original: βούλῃ σὺ μέντοι κοὐχὶ βουλήσῃ τάχα .
sections.translation: Though you want this now , you will not want it soon .

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: καὶ πῶς πατρῴαν γαῖαν οὐ σῷσαι θέλω ;
sections.translation: Not wish to save my country ? how can that be ?

sections.label: Section 901
sections.original: θέλεις ἀκοῦσαι δῆτα καὶ σπουδὴν ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Do you really wish to hear it , eagerly ?

sections.label: Section 902
sections.original: ἐς γὰρ τί μᾶλλον δεῖ προθυμίαν ἔχειν ;
sections.translation: Yes ; for where should I show greater zeal ?

sections.label: Section 903
sections.original: κλύοις ἂν ἤδη τῶν ἐμῶν θεσπισμάτων .
sections.translation: Then you will presently hear my prophetic words . But first I would know for certain

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: ποῦ ʼστιν Μενοικεύς , ὅς με δεῦρʼ ἐπήγαγεν ;
sections.translation: where Menoeceus is , who led me here .

sections.label: Section 906
sections.original: ὅδʼ οὐ μακρὰν ἄπεστι , πλησίον δὲ σοῦ .
sections.translation: Here , not far away , but at your side .

sections.label: Section 907
sections.original: ἀπελθέτω νυν θεσφάτων ἐμῶν ἑκάς .
sections.translation: Let him go far from my prophecies .

sections.label: Section 908
sections.original: ἐμὸς πεφυκὼς παῖς ἃ δεῖ σιγήσεται .
sections.translation: He is my own son and will be silent as he ought .

sections.label: Section 909
sections.original: βούλῃ παρόντος δῆτά σοι τούτου φράσω ;
sections.translation: Do you want me to tell you in his presence ?

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: κλύων γὰρ ἂν τέρποιτο τῆς σωτηρίας .
sections.translation: Yes , for he will rejoice to hear the means of safety .

sections.label: Section 911
sections.original: ἄκουε δή νυν θεσφάτων ἐμῶν ὁδόν ·
sections.translation: Then hear the intent of my oracle ; if you observe it , you will save the city of Cadmus you must sacrifice Menoeceus , your son here , for your country , since you yourself are calling on fate .

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: τί φῄς ; τίνʼ εἶπας τόνδε μῦθον , ὦ γέρον ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ? What is this you have said , old man ?

sections.label: Section 916
sections.original: ἅπερ πέφυκε , ταῦτα κἀνάγκη σὲ δρᾶν .
sections.translation: I have said what is , and you must do it .

sections.label: Section 917
sections.original: ὦ πολλὰ λέξας ἐν βραχεῖ χρόνῳ κακά .
sections.translation: O great evil , spoken so briefly !

sections.label: Section 918
sections.original: σοί γʼ , ἀλλὰ πατρίδι μεγάλα καὶ σωτήρια .
sections.translation: Evil to you , but to your country great salvation .

sections.label: Section 919
sections.original: οὐκ ἔκλυον , οὐκ ἤκουσα · χαιρέτω πόλις .
sections.translation: I did not hear ; I never listened ; I renounce my city !

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: ἁνὴρ ὅδʼ οὐκέθʼ αὑτός · ἐκνεύει πάλιν .
sections.translation: The man is no longer himself ; he is drawing back .

sections.label: Section 921
sections.original: χαίρων ἴθʼ · οὐ γὰρ σῶν με δεῖ μαντευμάτων .
sections.translation: Go in peace ; it is not your prophecy I need .

sections.label: Section 922
sections.original: ἀπόλωλεν ἡ ἀλήθειʼ , ἐπεὶ σὺ δυστυχεῖς ;
sections.translation: Is truth dead , because you are unfortunate ?

sections.label: Section 923
sections.original: ὦ πρός σε γονάτων καὶ γερασμίου τριχὸς —
sections.translation: By your knees and gray hair —

sections.label: Section 924
sections.original: τί προσπίτνεις με ; δυσφύλακτʼ αἰτῇ κακά .
sections.translation: Why implore me ? You are demanding evils that are hard to prevent .

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: σίγα · πόλει δὲ τούσδε μὴ λέξῃς λόγους .
sections.translation: Be silent ; do not tell the city your news .

sections.label: Section 926
sections.original: ἀδικεῖν κελεύεις μʼ · οὐ σιωπήσαιμεν ἄν .
sections.translation: You bid me to act unjustly ; I cannot be silent .

sections.label: Section 927
sections.original: τί δή με δράσεις ; παῖδά μου κατακτενεῖς ;
sections.translation: What will you do to me ? Kill my child ?

sections.label: Section 928
sections.original: ἄλλοις μελήσει ταῦτʼ , ἐμοὶ δʼ εἰρήσεται .
sections.translation: That is for others to decide ; it is for me to speak .

sections.label: Section 929
sections.original: ἐκ τοῦ δʼ ἐμοὶ τόδʼ ἦλθε καὶ τέκνῳ κακόν ;
sections.translation: How did this curse come on me and my son ?

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: ὀρθῶς μʼ ἐρωτᾷς κεἰς ἀγῶνʼ ἔρχῃ λόγων .
sections.translation: You do right to ask me and to test what I have said . In the chamber where the earth-born dragon kept watch over Dirce ’ s springs , he must be offered as a sacrifice and shed his blood on the ground , a libation of Cadmus , because of the ancient wrath of Ares ,

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: ὃς γηγενεῖ δράκοντι τιμωρεῖ φόνον .
sections.translation: who now avenges the slaughter of his earth-born snake . If you do this , you shall win Ares as an ally . If the earth receives fruit for fruit and human blood for blood , you shall find her kind to you again , who once

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: σπαρτῶν ἀνῆκεν · ἐκ γένους δὲ δεῖ θανεῖν
sections.translation: sent up to us a crop of Sown-men with golden helmets ; for one of those born from the dragon ’ s teeth must die .

sections.label: Section 942
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐνθάδʼ ἡμῖν λοιπὸς εἶ σπαρτῶν γένους
sections.translation: Now you are our only survivor of the Sown race , pure-blooded both on your mother ’ s and your father ’ s side , you and your sons . Haemon ’ s marriage

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: σφαγὰς ἀπείργουσʼ . οὐ γάρ ἐστιν ᾔθεος ·
sections.translation: holds him back from the slaughter , for he is no longer single ; even if he has not consummated his marriage , yet he is betrothed . But this tender youth , consecrated to his city , might by dying rescue his country ; and bitter will he make the return of Adrastus and his Argives ,

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: θήσει , μέλαιναν κῆρʼ ἐπʼ ὄμμασιν βαλών ,
sections.translation: flinging over their eyes a black spirit of death , and he will glorify Thebes . Choose one of these two destinies : either save the city or your son .

sections.label: Section 953
sections.original: τὰ μὲν παρʼ ἡμῶν πάντʼ ἔχεις · ἡγοῦ , τέκνον ,
sections.translation: Now you have all that I had to say . Daughter , lead me home . The man who practices the prophet ’ s art

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: μάταιος · ἢν μὲν ἐχθρὰ σημήνας τύχῃ ,
sections.translation: is a fool ; for if he happens to give an adverse answer , he makes himself disliked by those for whom he takes the omens ; while if he pities and deceives those who are consulting him , he wrongs the gods . Phoebus should have been man ’ s only prophet , for he fears no one . Exit Teiresias .

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: Κρέον , τί σιγᾷς γῆρυν ἄφθογγον σχάσας ;
sections.translation: Creon , why are you so silent , without a word ? I too am no less amazed .

sections.label: Section 962
sections.original: τί δʼ ἄν τις εἴποι ; δῆλον οἵ γʼ ἐμοὶ λόγοι .
sections.translation: What can one say ? It is clear what my words must be . For I will never come to such misfortune as to devote my son to death for the city ;

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: πᾶσιν γὰρ ἀνθρώποισι φιλότεκνος βίος ,
sections.translation: for all men love their children , and no one would give his own son to die . Let no man praise me , and kill my child at the same time . I myself , for I am in the prime of life , am ready to die to save my country .

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶα , τέκνον , πρὶν μαθεῖν πᾶσαν πόλιν ,
sections.translation: But come , my son , before the whole city learns this , fly with all haste away from this land , regardless of these prophets ’ reckless warnings ; for he will tell all this to our rulers and generals going to the seven gates and the captains ;

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: κἂν μὲν φθάσωμεν , ἔστι σοι σωτηρία ·
sections.translation: now if we can forestall him , you are saved , but if you are too late , we are ruined and you will die .

sections.label: Section 977
sections.original: ποῖ δῆτα φεύγω ; τίνα πόλιν ; τίνα ξένων ;
sections.translation: Where can I escape ? To what city ? To which of our guest-friends ?

sections.label: Section 978
sections.original: ὅπου χθονὸς τῆσδʼ ἐκποδὼν μάλιστʼ ἔσῃ .
sections.translation: Where you will be furthest removed from this land .

sections.label: Section 979
sections.original: οὐκοῦν σὲ φράζειν εἰκός , ἐκπονεῖν δʼ ἐμέ .
sections.translation: It is for you to name a place , for me to carry out your bidding .

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: Δελφοὺς περάσας —
sections.translation: After passing Delphi —

sections.label: Section 980b
sections.original: ποῖ με χρή , πάτερ , μολεῖν ;
sections.translation: Where must I go , father ?

sections.label: Section 981
sections.original: Αἰτωλίδʼ ἐς γῆν .
sections.translation: To Aetolia .
